title: Malaysian State Roads system

infobox.value: This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Malaysian_State_Roads_system "Special:EditPage/Malaysian State Roads system") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Malaysian State Roads system"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Malaysian+State+Roads+system%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Malaysian+State+Roads+system%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Malaysian+State+Roads+system%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Malaysian+State+Roads+system%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Malaysian+State+Roads+system%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Malaysian+State+Roads+system%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(February 2017)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))*

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Malaysian+State+Roads+system%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Malaysian+State+Roads+system%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Malaysian+State+Roads+system%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Malaysian+State+Roads+system%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(February 2017)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Sistem Laluan Negeri Malaysia*) is made up of the secondary roads in [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia"), with a total length of 264,906.73 km (as of December 2023).[[1]](#cite_note-foo-bar-1) The construction and maintenance works of state roads in Malaysia is managed by [Malaysian Public Works Department](/wiki/Malaysian_Public_Works_Department "Malaysian Public Works Department") (JKR) of each state and funded by state governments. The standard of the state roads is similar with the [federal roads](/wiki/Malaysian_Federal_Roads_System "Malaysian Federal Roads System") except for the coding system, where the codes for state roads begin with state codes followed by route number, for example [Johor State Route J32](/wiki/Johor_State_Route_J32 "Johor State Route J32") is labeled as J32. If a state road crosses the state border, the state code will change, for example route B20 in [Salak Tinggi](/wiki/Salak_Tinggi "Salak Tinggi"), [Selangor](/wiki/Selangor "Selangor") will change to N20 after crossing the border of [Negeri Sembilan](/wiki/Negeri_Sembilan "Negeri Sembilan") to [Nilai](/wiki/Nilai "Nilai") (Both B20 and N20 now become [Malaysia Federal Route 32](/wiki/Malaysia_Federal_Route_32 "Malaysia Federal Route 32")).

Malaysian Road Statistic 2024 by Public Works Department (JKR) Malaysia <https://www.jkr.gov.my/sites/default/files/upload/BUKU%20STATISTIK%20JALAN%20EDISI%202024_FINAL%20%281%29.pdf>[*[permanent dead link](/wiki/Wikipedia:Link_rot "Wikipedia:Link rot")*]

["Shah Alam Bandaraya Anggerik Part 2: Kenali sistem kod jalan perbandaran Shah Alam"](http://blogjalanraya.blogspot.my/2013/02/shah-alam-bandaraya-anggerik-part-2.html). *Blog Jalan Raya Malaysia*. Retrieved 15 May 2016.

Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Malaysian State Roads system"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Malaysian+State+Roads+system%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Malaysian+State+Roads+system%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Malaysian+State+Roads+system%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Malaysian+State+Roads+system%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Malaysian+State+Roads+system%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Malaysian+State+Roads+system%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(February 2017)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/30/K642_Road_1.jpg/250px-K642_Road_1.jpg)](/wiki/File:K642_Road_1.jpg)

A state route code sign in [Kedah](/wiki/Kedah "Kedah").

The **Malaysian state roads system** ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Sistem Laluan Negeri Malaysia*) is made up of the secondary roads in [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia"), with a total length of 264,906.73 km (as of December 2023).[[1]](#cite_note-foo-bar-1) The construction and maintenance works of state roads in Malaysia is managed by [Malaysian Public Works Department](/wiki/Malaysian_Public_Works_Department "Malaysian Public Works Department") (JKR) of each state and funded by state governments. The standard of the state roads is similar with the [federal roads](/wiki/Malaysian_Federal_Roads_System "Malaysian Federal Roads System") except for the coding system, where the codes for state roads begin with state codes followed by route number, for example [Johor State Route J32](/wiki/Johor_State_Route_J32 "Johor State Route J32") is labeled as J32. If a state road crosses the state border, the state code will change, for example route B20 in [Salak Tinggi](/wiki/Salak_Tinggi "Salak Tinggi"), [Selangor](/wiki/Selangor "Selangor") will change to N20 after crossing the border of [Negeri Sembilan](/wiki/Negeri_Sembilan "Negeri Sembilan") to [Nilai](/wiki/Nilai "Nilai") (Both B20 and N20 now become [Malaysia Federal Route 32](/wiki/Malaysia_Federal_Route_32 "Malaysia Federal Route 32")).

## List of state codes in Malaysian state roads system

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Malaysian_State_Roads_system&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: List of state codes in Malaysian state roads system")]

The codes assigned to each state is the same as those of the [car number plates](/wiki/Vehicle_registration_plates_of_Malaysia "Vehicle registration plates of Malaysia") except for Sabah.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/24/B35-signboard.JPG/330px-B35-signboard.JPG)](/wiki/File:B35-signboard.JPG)

Common state road signboard in Malaysia at [Bestari Jaya](/wiki/Bestari_Jaya "Bestari Jaya") (previously known as [Batang Berjuntai](/wiki/Batang_Berjuntai "Batang Berjuntai")), [Selangor](/wiki/Selangor "Selangor").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/72/Kampung_Titi_Akar_1.jpg/250px-Kampung_Titi_Akar_1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Kampung_Titi_Akar_1.jpg)

Jalan Kuala Ketil-Kuala Nerang (Central Spine Road; K17) near Titi Akar, Kedah.

* P: ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d4/Flag_of_Penang_%28Malaysia%29.svg/40px-Flag_of_Penang_%28Malaysia%29.svg.png) [Penang](/wiki/Penang "Penang")

## Municipal roads

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Malaysian_State_Roads_system&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Municipal roads")]

Municipal roads are usually not given a route number, as they are maintained mainly by the local councils. An exception is some major roads in [Shah Alam](/wiki/Shah_Alam "Shah Alam"), the capital of [Selangor](/wiki/Selangor "Selangor"). For example, Persiaran Raja Muda in Shah Alam was given route number BSA-3.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## List of state roads
